nodes.name: ContextPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Onboarding phase View — a browsable card carousel where the user swipes relationship-context cards, taps to confirm, and swipes up to commit the selection via VaylDirector.
nodes.languageNotes: Drives an injected CarouselPhysics value type and choreographs entrance/exit timelines with cancellable Tasks; all phase advancement routed through director.concludeContext.

nodes.name: ContextPhase
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View struct rendering the context-selection carousel; owns local animation state and forwards confirm/exit gestures to the director.

nodes.name: CredentialEditorSheet.swift
nodes.summary: In-canvas edit half-sheet (plus its custom CredentialEditorOverlay presenter) for revising a single onboarding credential during ConfirmationPhase, writing straight back to director.onboardingData.
nodes.languageNotes: Replaces the native .sheet (which insets in iOS 26) with a full-bleed in-canvas overlay using @Bindable two-way bindings into the director's data model.

nodes.name: CredentialEditorSheet
nodes.summary: Branched editor View that routes each OBCredential case (name, gender, mode, context, curiosity, etc.) to its own selectable-row editor bound to director.onboardingData.

nodes.name: CredentialEditorOverlay
nodes.summary: Custom partial-height bottom-sheet presenter hosting CredentialEditorSheet as a draggable, scrim-dismissable in-canvas overlay.

nodes.name: CuriosityPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Onboarding phase View rendering a two-round Tinder-style card-sort pile; the user sweeps left to discard or right to keep, with all sort state owned by director.curiosity.
nodes.languageNotes: Identity-stable ForEach keyed by card id so the next card rises into place; the forged-deck handoff uses shared HandBackFollow finger-tracking.

nodes.name: CuriosityPhase
nodes.summary: Near-pure renderer View for the curiosity card sort; forwards drag/commit gestures to director.curiosity and renders the squared-deck deal cascade.

nodes.name: DemoPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Onboarding phase View for the user's first card — a sentence-completion ("I [verb] [noun].") that teaches tap-to-lift and swipe-to-seal while triangulating an EmotionalRegister committed via director.commitDemoSnapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of a Canvas effects layer (impact ring, flip burst, particle dissolve seeds) plus a hidden TextField under a LivingText overlay for caret-aligned spectrum input.

nodes.name: DemoPhase
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View struct orchestrating the demo card's deal/flip/lift/compose/seal scene timeline and committing the derived snapshot before advancing to NamePhase.

nodes.name: ExperienceLevelPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Onboarding phase View dealing three candle cards into a fan via ThreeCardFanController; tap lifts to reveal a level, swipe-up confirms and writes nmStage through director.commitExperienceLevel.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges a SpriteKit SpriteView (deal-in flight) into SwiftUI cards, with a 30fps TimelineView capping candle-flicker redraws and rotation3DEffect edge-turn reveals.

nodes.name: ExperienceLevelPhase
nodes.summary: View struct driving the candle-card fan; owns a ThreeCardFanController and reacts to its state machine to ask the question, auto-select, and advance to context.

nodes.name: FounderLetterPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Final onboarding phase View presenting the founder letter sheet; the pull-down completion gesture commits the whole onboarding via director.finishOnboarding(using: onboardingStore).
nodes.languageNotes: Pull-down is the completion (not cancel) gesture; consumes OnboardingStore from the environment and embeds the AnimatedSignature draw-on.

nodes.name: FounderLetterPhase
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the founder letter and committing onboarding completion on a threshold pull-down, with a retry surface on commit failure.

nodes.name: GenderPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Onboarding phase View where a radio-tuner card crystallises from the felt and the user tunes gender + pronoun drums; near-pure renderer with all visual state on director.gender.
nodes.languageNotes: Twin scrollable drum pickers driven by manual offset math; a shared 'prefer not to say' decline bar clears both dials; confirm uses HandBackFollow hand-off tracking.

nodes.name: GenderPhase
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the gender/pronoun tuner card and drum pickers; forwards tune/lift/confirm gestures to director.gender (the GenderSequencer).

nodes.name: ModeSelectPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Onboarding phase View dealing two solo/together cards from opposite sides via CardMirrorDealController; the user lifts one and swipes up to set appMode and advance to gender.
nodes.languageNotes: Cards are intentionally label-free (controller illustrations are the signifier); includes a playful cheat-code button animation and couples-first default auto-lift.

nodes.name: ModeSelectPhase
nodes.summary: View struct driving the mirror-deal solo-vs-together card pick; owns a CardMirrorDealController and writes director.onboardingData.appMode on confirm.

nodes.name: NamePhase.swift
nodes.summary: The flagship onboarding phase View — deals and flips the first card, runs the typewriter name input and greeting, and teaches the universal tap-lift then swipe-up hand-back gesture lesson.
nodes.languageNotes: The largest phase (937 lines): a multi-beat dealer/typewriter choreography with its own CardDealPhase state machine, Canvas effects, and the canonical lift/hand-off tutorial reused by every later phase.

nodes.name: NamePhase
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the deal/flip/name-input/greeting/hand-back sequence and teaching the core OB gestures, advancing via director on card return.

nodes.name: SingleGreetingSheet.swift
nodes.summary: An 'honest moment' overlay (SingleGreetingOverlay) shown when a user confirms 'I'm single' in ContextPhase, acknowledged via 'Got it' which commits the pending conclusion through director.continueFromSingleGreeting.

nodes.name: SingleGreetingOverlay
nodes.summary: Scrimmed bottom-sheet overlay View presenting the couples-first single-user message and the 'Got it' continue button.

nodes.name: StatPhase.swift
nodes.summary: Early onboarding phase View presenting the '1 in 5' hero statistic with a holographic ignition entrance cascade and a citation expander, advancing on CTA.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves a responsive hero numeral size from AppLayout and reads realSafeArea from the environment so canvas and preview contexts share identical usable height.

nodes.name: StatPhase
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the holographic statistic hero, entrance cascade, and citation; advances the director on the CTA.

nodes.name: AnimatedSignature.swift
nodes.summary: Native SwiftUI draw-on of the founder's three-stroke handwritten signature, each pen-stroke trim-animated with lift pauses and rendered in glow plus crisp spectrum passes.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-stroke trim progress state with pen-lift cadence; follows the OB two-render-pass rule (blurred spectrum glow + crisp line).

nodes.name: AnimatedSignature
nodes.summary: Self-contained View that plays the founder signature draw-on once on appear via independent per-stroke trim animations.

nodes.name: DealPointView.swift
nodes.summary: Stateless render of the OB deal point — the table-horizon origin from which all onboarding cards launch, its glow intensity driven by VaylDirector.

nodes.name: ProjectedTextView.swift
nodes.summary: Stateless render of the dealer's voice — text typed character-by-character onto the felt at the table horizon, driven by VaylDirector's projected text state.
nodes.languageNotes: Center-grow mode reveals the real typed substring in a static full-width frame; non-center mode paints unrevealed characters clear to keep wrap geometry stable mid-type.

nodes.name: ProjectedTextView
nodes.summary: View struct that types dealer copy onto the felt at the shared cadence with a projection-entrance grow, never holding gesture state.

nodes.name: OnboardingStore.swift
nodes.summary: Observable MainActor store that commits completed OnboardingData to SwiftData, persisting every field onto UserProfile and mirroring displayName/appMode into AppState.
nodes.languageNotes: Creates a fresh ModelContext at write time (never stored on self); AppState.markOnboardingComplete is the single completion writer.

nodes.name: OnboardingStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor store owning onboarding-completion state; commit() persists data and marks completion, with persist() and mirrorIntoAppState() helpers.

nodes.name: OnboardingError
nodes.summary: LocalizedError enum for onboarding-commit failures (save failure or incomplete data).

nodes.name: commit
nodes.summary: Gates on data readiness, persists the profile, mirrors into AppState, and marks onboarding complete; returns false and records the error on failure.

nodes.name: persist
nodes.summary: Fetches or creates the UserProfile in a fresh ModelContext and writes every OnboardingData field, throwing on save failure.

nodes.name: PairingInviteView.swift
nodes.summary: Person-A pairing View that generates an invite code and waits for a partner to join; display-only with all logic delegated to PairingStore.

nodes.name: PairingInviteView
nodes.summary: View struct showing generating/waiting/expired/error states for the invite flow, driving PairingStore.generateInvite and rendering the code.

nodes.name: PairingJoinView.swift
nodes.summary: Person-B pairing View where the user enters a partner's code to join; display-only with all logic delegated to PairingStore.joinWithCode.

nodes.name: PairingJoinView
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the code-entry field and join states, validating a 6+ character code before calling PairingStore.

nodes.name: PairingSettingsView.swift
nodes.summary: Partner-linking settings screen showing current link state and surfacing the invite/join actions when unlinked, reading link state from AppState.
nodes.languageNotes: Hosts the invite and join flows in a NavigationStack and reads AppState.linkState from the environment.

nodes.name: PairingSettingsView
nodes.summary: View struct presenting link-state and action sections, gating actions on the unlinked state and navigating to invite/join views.

nodes.name: PairingStore.swift
nodes.summary: Observable MainActor store owning the device-pairing flow — generate/regenerate/join/poll lifecycle over PairingService, persisting the couple link to SwiftData and mirroring it into AppState.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a PairingLinkState enum with a manual Equatable conformance; polls every 3s with a cancellable Task and back-fills partner identity via SyncManager.

nodes.name: PairingStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor store driving the pairing state machine via PairingService and persisting the linked coupleId to UserProfile and AppState.

nodes.name: PairingLinkState
nodes.summary: Equatable enum modeling the pairing lifecycle: idle, generating, waitingForPartner, joining, linked, and error.

nodes.name: generateInvite
nodes.summary: Person-A flow: pushes identity, requests a code from PairingService, moves to waitingForPartner, and begins polling for the partner.

nodes.name: joinWithCode
nodes.summary: Person-B flow: validates and claims a code via PairingService, persists the resulting link, and refreshes partner identity.

nodes.name: pollForPartner
nodes.summary: Polls PairingService until the partner claims the code or it expires, persisting the link and handling cancellation/expiry/error.

nodes.name: persistLink
nodes.summary: Writes coupleId/isLinked/linkedAt to UserProfile in a fresh ModelContext and mirrors the link into AppState.

nodes.name: PlayView.swift
nodes.summary: Placeholder Play tab View; in DEBUG builds it hosts a temporary stub-session button that exercises the curated-session insert path against Supabase.
nodes.languageNotes: The debug path uses AuthService, ProfileService, and RealtimeSessionService directly under #if DEBUG; the production tab is currently a title placeholder.

nodes.name: PlayView
nodes.summary: Play tab View struct with a debug-only stub-session opener that resolves the couple and inserts a curated session for runtime verification.

nodes.name: ProgressDashboardView.swift
nodes.summary: Progress dashboard View showing exploration score, per-category breakdown, and recent activity, sourced from DataStore's streak and session records.
nodes.languageNotes: Currently rebuilds DataStore from the modelContext on each access (flagged TODO to inject via init); screenshot-protected.

nodes.name: ProgressDashboardView
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the progress header, score ring, category breakdown, and recent activity from DataStore queries.

nodes.name: CheckInShell.swift
nodes.summary: Full-screen container for the Pulse check-in: PulseGraph fills the top 60% (always visible) while DailyCheckInView slides up in the bottom 40%, with camera bindings owned by PulseWidget.
nodes.languageNotes: Owns the background and glow field (AuroraGlowField/OnboardingAtmosphere by color scheme) and threads camera + live-score bindings down to DailyCheckInView.

nodes.name: CheckInShell
nodes.summary: View struct composing the always-visible PulseGraph and the DailyCheckInView panel over a shared atmosphere background.

nodes.name: DailyCheckInView.swift
nodes.summary: The 5-question daily check-in panel rendered inside CheckInShell; each pill answer moves liveScore so the live dot animates on the graph above, returning a PulseEntry on completion.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a CheckInPhase state machine plus private CheckInQuestion/CheckInPill models; renders no background of its own (CheckInShell owns it) and drives the graph camera via bindings.

nodes.name: DailyCheckInView
nodes.summary: View struct sequencing the check-in questions, animating the live dot via camera bindings, and emitting a PulseEntry to the owner.

nodes.name: PulseCanvasScrollView.swift
nodes.summary: Pure SwiftUI two-axis ScrollView wrapper for PulseGraph that anchors to the most-recent entry and locks the outer dashboard scroll while a touch is on the graph.

nodes.name: PulseCanvasScrollView
nodes.summary: View struct embedding PulseGraph in a scrollable canvas, anchoring bottom-trailing on appear and exposing an isGraphActive scroll lock.

nodes.name: PulseDotSummary.swift
nodes.summary: Overlay View presenting a single Pulse entry's detail via a spectrum 'burn' that expands from the tapped dot, with ember particles and a smart above/below flip; dismiss collapses the burn back into the dot.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a private Ember particle model and adapts its burn color and scrim to color scheme (full spectrum in dark, warm aurora in light).

nodes.name: PulseDotSummary
nodes.summary: View struct rendering the expanding spectrum burn, floating entry info, and ember particle field over PulseGraph, collapsing on dismiss.
